name: PHYS_MEM_MAP_Gigabyte_gdrv_MOAD_shape
notes: Gigabyte gdrv.sys (MOAD, CVE-2018-19320) PHYS_MEM_MAP cluster: device_type 0xC350 dispatcher chains MmMapIoSpace (caller-controlled PA/length, MmNonCached forced) + HalTranslateBusAddress (5-arg lowering via [rsp+20h] TranslatedAddress) + ZwOpenSection(\Device\PhysicalMemory) + ZwMapViewOfSection + MmGetPhysicalAddress in a single MJ14 handler with ZERO gates (no IoCreateDeviceSecure, no SDDL, no ExGetPreviousMode, no ProbeForRead/Write, no SE_*_PRIVILEGE, no magic cookie). Sequence 1 catches inline MJ14 MmMapIoSpace lowering (xor r8d,r8d ; mov rdx,rbp ; call cs:imp). Sequence 2 catches the sub_11570 wrapper variant (mov edx,[rdi+14h] length, mov rcx,[rdi+8] PA, xor r8d,r8d). Sequence 3 catches HalTranslateBusAddress 5-arg lowering (mov edx,r12d BusNumber, mov ecx,ebp InterfaceType, mov [rsp+20h],rax TranslatedAddress). Sequence 4 catches MmGetPhysicalAddress(rdi=BaseAddress) followed by DbgPrint lea. Reference driver self-test on gdrv.sys: 5 byte-pattern hits across 4 sequences, both positive_strings present, all 5 import_required present, zero import_forbidden, zero negative_strings.
